crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Bank credit to grow ~13% this fiscal, a smidgen slower on-year
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Bank credit  is poised to grow ~13% this fiscal, driven by healthy growth in the micro, small and medium enterprise (MSME) and retail sectors, as well as the continued preference of corporates for bank credit rather than issuance of bonds amid the prevailing interest rate differential.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Sunflower oil consumption set to wilt ~10% amid geopolitical heat
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Indian refined sunflower oil volume is poised to decline ~10% in the current fiscal, due to twin headwinds that will dampen demand. Firstly, supply-chain disruptions triggered by the Middle East conflict, and secondly, higher prices resulting from the pass-through of increasing logistics costs, will

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Credit ratio of India Inc moderates to 1.50 in the second half
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The Crisil Ratings credit ratio, or the proportion of rating upgrades to downgrades, stood at 1.50 times in the second half of fiscal 2026, moderating from 2.17 times in the first half.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Cumulative recovery rate for ARCs to rise to ~90% next fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Asset reconstruction companies (ARCs) are expected to see the cumulative recovery rate  of security receipts (SRs) go up by ~700 basis points in the upcoming fiscal, taking the total to ~90%. Healthy performance and resolution of chunkier stressed assets in key infrastructure sectors such as real es

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Residential realty sales growth to moderate slightly to 4-6% in fiscal 2027
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The residential real estate industry appears to have entered a phase of calibrated growth after a strong post-pandemic surge that saw it clock a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 26% in sales (value) from fiscal 2022 to fiscal 2025.

crisil-research.title: RRR: Roti Rice Rate
crisil-research.description: In March, the cost of a home-cooked vegetarian (veg) thali was flat on-year, while that of a non-vegetarian (non-veg) thali fell 1%.

crisil-research.description: The BDC Finance division of a private markets manager required support in regulatory reporting and audit documentation. It primarily involved 10Q/10K reports required to be filed with SEC, within the stipulated period.

crisil-research.title: Readthrough of a hawkish BoE
crisil-research.description: The Bank of England’s (BoE) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has cautioned that a prolonged period of inflationary pressure caused by the conflict in the Middle East and the resultant weakening of economic activity could trigger second-round effects in wages and prices.

crisil-research.title: Predictive cyber resilience in an AI-driven world

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: A tad higher
crisil-research.description: The Index of Industrial Production (IIP) edged up to 5.2% on-year in February from 5.1% in January, driven by stronger growth in manufacturing (6% vs 5.3%) but partially offset by slowdown in electricity (2.3% vs 5.1%) and mining (3.1% vs 4.3%).
